title: Grupo G - AI Services & Process Diagnosis Proposal Review
summary: Grupo G's directors met with the AI consulting team (Daniel & Daniel Restrepo) to evaluate their AI transformation proposal. The group expressed strong interest in Plan A (full process diagnosis at $6,000) to map all organizational processes and identify quick-win automation opportunities. Key concerns were raised around source code ownership, hosting model, NDA requirements, and database architecture strategy. The team agreed to run the diagnosis and a proof-of-concept (POC) in parallel, with the consulting team to send an updated proposal addressing ownership options and cost structures.

discussion_points.topic: Plan A vs Plan B Service Approach
discussion_points.description: Plan A is a comprehensive process diagnosis ($6,000) involving WhatsApp interviews with key staff to map all organizational processes, identify friction points, and find quick-win opportunities ('low-hanging fruit'). Plan B targets specific known pain points with custom AI solutions. The group favors Plan A but wants both to run in parallel.

discussion_points.topic: Invoicing Complexity as Primary Pain Point
discussion_points.description: Grupo G's invoicing process is highly complex and matrix-like: each client requires different billing for routes, warehousing, maquila (assembly), and transport. Multiple teams review and re-review before invoicing, leading to errors, client rejections, re-billing, and delayed collections. This was identified as the most critical operational bottleneck.

Héctor raised concerns about who owns the developed tools. The consulting team explained the default model retains source code ownership with them, but offered to quote both options (client-owned vs consultant-owned code). Hosting can be on either party's infrastructure. The group wants both pricing structures in the formal proposal.

A live demo was shown of a custom quotation system built for a Colombian logistics company. It reduced quotation time from 7-10 days to 15 minutes, with route configuration, regulatory integration for legal minimums, statistical market analysis, and QR-code-based client acceptance. Built in 24 days (vs 6-8 months traditional), costing $8,000-$10,000 one-time plus recurring usage fees.

A pre-built bank reconciliation tool was demonstrated that matched ~90% of transactions in 5 minutes (vs 2-3 days manually). Priced at $4,000 one-time plus ~$150/month recurring. Less customization needed than quotation tools since back-office financial processes are more standardized.

Héctor requested that the proposal include recommendations for structuring the company's database to identify core revenue-generating processes and reduce administrative overhead. The consulting team described their approach: diagnose data flows between systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle), design a data warehouse architecture where systems feed data automatically without manual Excel manipulation.

A revenue intelligence dashboard was shown from a US freight forwarder engagement. It included client health scoring, quote-to-execution effectiveness rates, churn analysis, hidden gems identification, pipeline effectiveness by sales rep, payment risk assessment, and strategic revenue impact modeling (e.g., 5% churn reduction = $375K revenue).
Héctor emphasized the need for an NDA before sharing operational data, especially since the consulting team may work with other logistics companies. Daniel's team agreed to send the NDA.

discussion_points.topic: Existing PwC Process Map from 10 Years Ago
discussion_points.description: Héctor mentioned that PricewaterhouseCoopers created a process map for the company approximately 10 years ago (held by Pastor Oropesa). While useful as a baseline reference, all parties agreed it likely doesn't reflect current operations and should serve only as a guide, not a constraint for the new diagnosis.

Grupo G's tech team is limited to basic IT support (infrastructure, networks) with no product development capability. This reinforces the outsourced development model, as even if source code were transferred, the internal team couldn't maintain it. The consulting team's vision is that future business teams will be empowered to use and enable technology without a separate IT department.

The consulting team mentioned a revenue share model used with another client, where they participate in revenue increases generated by their initiatives. This was presented as an alternative engagement model alongside fixed-fee development and recurring subscription fees.

action_items.description: Send updated proposal with: (1) diagnosis deliverables, timeline, and cost; (2) code ownership options (client-owned vs consultant-owned) with pricing for each; (3) database architecture recommendations scope; (4) ongoing support/maintenance structure details
Send NDA to Grupo G for signing before data sharing begins
Locate and share the PricewaterhouseCoopers process map from ~10 years ago as baseline reference material
Identify a specific process for the initial proof-of-concept (invoicing or quotation recommended)
Send follow-up questions to Grupo G about current systems, team structure, and operations

decisions: Grupo G favors Plan A (full process diagnosis) to map all organizational processes before targeting specific solutions
Diagnosis and proof-of-concept (POC) development will run in parallel rather than sequentially
The proposal must include both pricing structures: client-owned source code vs consultant-owned source code
An NDA must be signed before operational data is shared with the consulting team
Database architecture recommendations will be included in the diagnosis scope
The existing PwC process map will be used only as a reference guide, not as the authoritative baseline
next_steps: Consulting team sends updated proposal with detailed cost structures, ownership options, and scope
Consulting team sends NDA for Grupo G to review and sign
Grupo G locates the PwC process map (Pastor Oropesa) and shares it as reference material
Grupo G selects a specific process (invoicing or quotation) for the initial proof-of-concept
Once NDA is signed and proposal accepted, WhatsApp-based interviews begin with key personnel (30-45 min each)
Diagnosis report expected within ~1 month; functional POC expected within ~2 weeks of starting development
Follow-up meeting to review diagnosis findings and prioritize automation opportunities

diagrams.title: Proposed Engagement Model - Parallel Workstreams
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    A["NDA Signed & Proposal Accepted"] --> B["Workstream 1: Process Diagnosis"]
    A --> C["Workstream 2: Proof of Concept"]
    B --> B1["WhatsApp Interviews\n30-45 min per person"]
    B1 --> B2["Data Analysis\n1.5-2 weeks"]
    B2 --> B3["Diagnosis Report\nProcess Maps & Recommendations"]
    B3 --> B4["Database Architecture\nRecommendation"]
    C --> C1["Discovery Call\nScope Definition"]
    C1 --> C2["Sprint 1: Build\n2 weeks"]
    C2 --> C3["User Testing\n& Feedback"]
    C3 --> C4["Sprint 2: Iterate\n2 weeks"]
    B4 --> D["Prioritized Roadmap\nQuick Wins Identified"]
    C4 --> D
    D --> E["Phase 2: Targeted\nAI Solutions Development"]

diagrams.title: Quotation System Architecture (Tenderi Case Study)
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart LR
    A["Excel Upload\nRoute Config"] --> B["Pricing Engine"]
    B --> C["Regulatory API\nLegal Minimums"]
    B --> D["Statistical Analysis\nMarket Rates"]
    C --> E["Cost Calculation\nFloor Price + Margin"]
    D --> E
    E --> F["Quotation Generated"]
    F --> G["Email with QR Code\nSent to Client"]
    G --> H["Client Portal"]
    H --> I{"Client Decision"}
    I -->|Accept| J["Order Confirmed"]
    I -->|Reject| K["Negotiation / Re-quote"]

diagrams.title: Grupo G Logistics Operations Flow - Three Pillars
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph DOC["Documents Flow"]
        D1["Client Request"] --> D2["Quotation Created"]
        D2 --> D3["Price Negotiation\nCommercial vs Operations"]
        D3 --> D4["Client Acceptance"]
        D4 --> D5["Service Documents\nRemesas, Photos, Proof"]
        D5 --> D6["Document Checklist\nPer Client Config"]
    end
    subgraph MONEY["Money Flow"]
        M1["Pricing Defined"] --> M2["Payment to Carrier\nor Operator"]
        M2 --> M3["Service Execution"]
        M3 --> M4["Invoice Generation\nMultiple Line Items"]
        M4 --> M5["Client Payment\n30-60-90-120 days"]
    end
    subgraph MERCH["Merchandise Flow"]
        G1["Pickup at Origin"] --> G2["Transport / Warehousing\n/ Maquila"]
        G2 --> G3["Delivery Confirmation"]
        G3 --> G4["Proof of Delivery\nDocumental Compliance"]
    end
    DOC --> RECONCILE["Month-End Reconciliation\nHow much moved, to whom,\nhow to bill"]
    MONEY --> RECONCILE
    MERCH --> RECONCILE

    H --> I["Collection\n30-60-90-120 days"]
    I --> J["Revenue Recognition"]

diagrams.title: Bank Reconciliation Process - Before vs After
diagrams.mermaid_code:
flowchart TD
    subgraph BEFORE["Before: Manual Process"]
        B1["Download Bank Statement"] --> B2["Open Accounting Records"]
        B2 --> B3["Manual Row-by-Row\nMatching in Excel"]
        B3 --> B4["Flag Discrepancies"]
        B4 --> B5["Investigate Mismatches"]
        B5 --> B6["Reconciliation Report"]
        B1 -.->|"2-3 Days\n1-2 People"| B6
    end
    subgraph AFTER["After: AI-Powered"]
        A1["Upload Bank Statement"] --> A2["AI Matching Engine\n90% Auto-Matched"]
        A2 --> A3["Review Unmatched\n10% Manual"]
        A3 --> A4["Reconciliation Report"]
        A1 -.->|"5 Minutes"| A4
    end
